item.text: {"approval_state":"proposed","bottleneck":"The workflow now has a matched stride-control pair at one 2048 KB working-set size, but it still cannot test whether the bandwidth gap is primarily a capacity/locality transition versus a stride-only effect because no second working-set point has been measured under the same hostile stride.","continue_recommended":true,"expected_gain":"A single smaller-working-set rerun at the same 128-byte stride would add an orthogonal comparison axis and make the locality interpretation more defensible without changing the benchmark family, controls, or append-only workflow.","proposal":"Run one rollbackable follow-up spec in the existing `cuda_memory_hierarchy_probe` handler with identical launch parameters and deterministic controls, keeping `stride_bytes=128` but reducing `working_set_kb` to a cache-plausible size such as 256 KB. Analyze it only as a paired capacity test against `spec_3626106571cf` and the accepted 4-byte-stride control `spec_ea7294e5fd1a`: if bandwidth rises materially versus the 2048 KB, 128-byte-stride point, record a narrower claim that performance is sensitive to working-set size under hostile stride; if it does not, keep the current conclusion limited to stride-conditioned observations. Preserve all stages and append-only history, and do not promote any cache-level attribution from a single added point.","proposed_profile_updates":[{"context_sections":["question","spec","claim","verification"],"instruction_note":"When proposing a next spec after a successful matched control, require the agent to name the next comparison axis explicitly (`stride`, `working_set`, or `launch_shape`) and cite the prior spec ids it will hold constant.","reason":"This makes later human review of experimental intent easier and reduces under-specified follow-up runs.","role":"spec_generator","sandbox_mode":"read-only","web_search_enabled":false},{"context_sections":["spec","claim","verification"],"instruction_note":"For memory-hierarchy claims, allow cache-level language only after at least one controlled working-set comparison is present; otherwise label results as locality- or capacity-sensitive observations only.","reason":"This preserves rigor while keeping curation aligned with the evidence actually collected.","role":"analyzer","sandbox_mode":"read-only","web_search_enabled":false}],"risk":"A 256 KB point may still be ambiguous if it does not clearly separate cache-resident from non-resident behavior, so the added run could yield weak evidence without fully resolving the memory-level interpretation.","rollback_condition":"If the added 128-byte-stride working-set comparison cannot be executed deterministically within the 120-second budget, produces incompatible metadata, or shows no stable signal on verification, retain the current accepted pair of single-condition claims and do not elevate the locality interpretation beyond the existing matched-stride comparison.","stop_reason":null,"target_workflow_version":"workflow_v1"}
